headline: ServiceNow (NOW) Is Raising Its AI Targets As Investors Shift Toward Software
summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) is seeing investors rotate capital from AI infrastructure stocks toward application-layer software. The company recently raised its Now Assist AI contract target, signaling stronger focus on monetizing AI within its platform. Early indications suggest ServiceNow is positioning AI as a premium add-on across its workflow products. ServiceNow sits at the heart of enterprise workflows, and its Now Assist AI tools are becoming more central as companies look to coordinate...

headline: Software stocks tumble: High memory prices cannibalize enterprise tech capex
summary: Tech stocks faced downward pressure Tuesday as disappointing preliminary results from IBM (NYSE:IBM) rippled across the sector, pulling shares of major software companies lower. Following IBM’s update, investors reacted sharply to the cooling sentiment in the broader software space: Accenture (NYSE:ACN): Fell 8% ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW): Dropped 6% Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY): Declined 5% Salesforce (NYSE:CRM): Slipped 5% The decline is rooted in a fundamental shift in enterprise capital expenditure (cap

headline: The Detail in IBM’s Earnings Pre-Announcement That Has Stock Headed for Worst Day in Nearly 40 Years
summary: IBM  stock headed for its largest same-day plunge in nearly 40 years on Tuesday after the company’s preliminary second-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue missed analysts’ targets.  In a letter to shareholders, CEO Arvind Krishna explained that the shortfall was largely driven by the company’s infrastructure business.  “What played out was worse than our expectations,” Krishna wrote.

content.title: The Detail in IBM’s Earnings Pre-Announcement That Has Stock Headed for Worst Day in Nearly 40 Years
content.summary: IBM  stock headed for its largest same-day plunge in nearly 40 years on Tuesday after the company’s preliminary second-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue missed analysts’ targets.  In a letter to shareholders, CEO Arvind Krishna explained that the shortfall was largely driven by the company’s infrastructure business.  “What played out was worse than our expectations,” Krishna wrote.

headline: C1Secure Launches SmartRAMP 20x, a ServiceNow-Native Platform Built for the FedRAMP 20x Model
summary: C1Secure today announced SmartRAMP 20x, a ServiceNow-native application built for FedRAMP 20x, the federal government's new model for certifying cloud services. As a ServiceNow Build Partner, C1Secure delivers SmartRAMP 20x on the ServiceNow AI Platform, giving cloud service providers a faster, lower-cost path to the FedRAMP Marketplace and giving agencies a live, evidence-based view of compliance for every service they authorize. SmartRAMP 20x is planned for publication on the ServiceNow Store.

content.title: C1Secure Launches SmartRAMP 20x, a ServiceNow-Native Platform Built for the FedRAMP 20x Model
content.summary: C1Secure today announced SmartRAMP 20x, a ServiceNow-native application built for FedRAMP 20x, the federal government's new model for certifying cloud services. As a ServiceNow Build Partner, C1Secure delivers SmartRAMP 20x on the ServiceNow AI Platform, giving cloud service providers a faster, lower-cost path to the FedRAMP Marketplace and giving agencies a live, evidence-based view of compliance for every service they authorize. SmartRAMP 20x is planned for publication on the ServiceNow Store.

headline: IBM Tumbles 22% Toward Its Worst Day Since 1987, Rattling Software Stocks
summary: IBM just logged its worst single-session collapse in nearly four decades, and the shockwaves are already hitting Microsoft, ServiceNow, and the broader software sector in ways that raise an uncomfortable question about where enterprise spending is actually going.

content.title: IBM Tumbles 22% Toward Its Worst Day Since 1987, Rattling Software Stocks
content.summary: IBM just logged its worst single-session collapse in nearly four decades, and the shockwaves are already hitting Microsoft, ServiceNow, and the broader software sector in ways that raise an uncomfortable question about where enterprise spending is actually going.

headline: Latest News In Cloud AI - AI Revolutionizes Underwriting with Novacore and FurtherAI Partnership
summary: FurtherAI, an AI workspace tailored for the insurance sector, has been chosen by Novacore as the backbone of its underwriting operations. The integration aims to enhance Novacore's efficiency by replacing outdated systems with a platform that molds to specific underwriting workflows, enabling flexibility and growth with evolving AI models. This partnership underscores a trend in Cloud AI deployment within the insurance industry, with AI-driven tools becoming central to streamlined operations...

This partnership underscores a trend in Cloud AI deployment within the insurance industry, with AI-driven tools becoming central to streamlined operations...

headline: The Detail in IBM’s Earnings Pre-Announcement That Has The Stock Headed for Its Worst Day Ever
summary: IBM  stock was pacing toward its worst day ever on Tuesday after the company’s preliminary second-quarter earnings missed analysts’ targets.  In a letter to shareholders, CEO Arvind Krishna explained that the shortfall was largely driven by the company’s infrastructure business.  “What played out was worse than our expectations,” Krishna wrote.
